thought.text: The data has step-level granularity with error tracking. Let me see if there's richer content in the text field and look for trials with actual success/failure variance:

action.command:
cd /workspace/project/tb-difficulty-tuner && export WANDB_API_KEY="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" && export PINECONE_API_KEY="pcsk_3Vsf8s_UoX9xAA4AzvCu6kJFoAYxMs33WKzL3VdtgMpK2tSQUodh3oVBM62BnSZn22Zgzr" && python3 << 'EOF'
import os
from pinecone import Pinecone
from collections import defaultdict

pc = Pinecone(api_key=os.environ["PINECONE_API_KEY"])
index = pc.Index("agent-task-benchmark")

# Get ALL trials to analyze
all_trials = []
for ids in index.list(namespace="trials"):
    all_trials.extend(ids)

print(f"Total trials in index: {len(all_trials)}")

# Fetch all trials in batches
all_metadata = {}
batch_size = 100
for i in range(0, len(all_trials), batch_size):
    batch = all_trials[i:i+batch_size]
    fetched = index.fetch(ids=batch, namespace="trials")
    for tid, vec in fetched.vectors.items():
        all_metadata[tid] = vec.metadata

# Analyze what we have
print(f"\nFetched metadata for {len(all_metadata)} trials")

# Group by task and analyze pass/fail
task_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {"passed": 0, "failed": 0, "agents": set(), "trials": []})
agent_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {"passed": 0, "failed": 0})

for tid, meta in all_metadata.items():
    task = meta.get("task_name", "unknown")
    agent = meta.get("agent_name", "unknown")
    passed = meta.get("passed", False)
    
    task_stats[task]["trials"].append(tid)
    if passed:
        task_stats[task]["passed"] += 1
        agent_stats[agent]["passed"] += 1
    else:
        task_stats[task]["failed"] += 1
        agent_stats[agent]["failed"] += 1
    task_stats[task]["agents"].add(agent)

print("\n=== AGENT PERFORMANCE ===")
for agent, stats in sorted(agent_stats.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]["passed"], reverse=True):
    total = stats["passed"] + stats["failed"]
    rate = stats["passed"] / total if total > 0 else 0
    print(f"  {agent}: {stats['passed']}/{total} ({rate:.1%})")

print("\n=== TASKS WITH VARIANCE (interesting for reward learning) ===")
variance_tasks = []
for task, stats in sorted(task_stats.items()):
    if stats["passed"] > 0 and stats["failed"] > 0:  # Has both pass and fail
        total = stats["passed"] + stats["failed"]
        rate = stats["passed"] / total
        variance_tasks.append((task, stats["passed"], stats["failed"], rate, stats["agents"]))

for task, p, f, rate, agents in sorted(variance_tasks, key=lambda x: x[3]):  # sort by pass rate
    print(f"  {task}: {p} pass, {f} fail ({rate:.1%}) - agents: {agents}")

print(f"\n=== SUMMARY ===")
print(f"Tasks with variance (both pass & fail): {len(variance_tasks)}")
print(f"Tasks with ONLY passes: {sum(1 for t, s in task_stats.items() if s['passed'] > 0 and s['failed'] == 0)}")
print(f"Tasks with ONLY fails: {sum(1 for t, s in task_stats.items() if s['passed'] == 0 and s['failed'] > 0)}")
EOF

import os
from pinecone import Pinecone
from collections import defaultdict

pc = Pinecone(api_key=os.environ["PINECONE_API_KEY"])
index = pc.Index("agent-task-benchmark")

# Get ALL trials to analyze
all_trials = []
for ids in index.list(namespace="trials"):
    all_trials.extend(ids)

print(f"Total trials in index: {len(all_trials)}")

# Fetch all trials in batches
all_metadata = {}
batch_size = 100
for i in range(0, len(all_trials), batch_size):
    batch = all_trials[i:i+batch_size]
    fetched = index.fetch(ids=batch, namespace="trials")
    for tid, vec in fetched.vectors.items():
        all_metadata[tid] = vec.metadata

# Analyze what we have
print(f"\nFetched metadata for {len(all_metadata)} trials")

# Group by task and analyze pass/fail
task_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {"passed": 0, "failed": 0, "agents": set(), "trials": []})
agent_stats = defaultdict(lambda: {"passed": 0, "failed": 0})

for tid, meta in all_metadata.items():
    task = meta.get("task_name", "unknown")
    agent = meta.get("agent_name", "unknown")
    passed = meta.get("passed", False)
    
    task_stats[task]["trials"].append(tid)
    if passed:
        task_stats[task]["passed"] += 1
        agent_stats[agent]["passed"] += 1
    else:
        task_stats[task]["failed"] += 1
        agent_stats[agent]["failed"] += 1
    task_stats[task]["agents"].add(agent)

print("\n=== AGENT PERFORMANCE ===")
for agent, stats in sorted(agent_stats.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]["passed"], reverse=True):
    total = stats["passed"] + stats["failed"]
    rate = stats["passed"] / total if total > 0 else 0
    print(f"  {agent}: {stats['passed']}/{total} ({rate:.1%})")

print("\n=== TASKS WITH VARIANCE (interesting for reward learning) ===")
variance_tasks = []
for task, stats in sorted(task_stats.items()):
    if stats["passed"] > 0 and stats["failed"] > 0:  # Has both pass and fail
        total = stats["passed"] + stats["failed"]
        rate = stats["passed"] / total
        variance_tasks.append((task, stats["passed"], stats["failed"], rate, stats["agents"]))

for task, p, f, rate, agents in sorted(variance_tasks, key=lambda x: x[3]):  # sort by pass rate
    print(f"  {task}: {p} pass, {f} fail ({rate:.1%}) - agents: {agents}")

print(f"\n=== SUMMARY ===")
print(f"Tasks with variance (both pass & fail): {len(variance_tasks)}")
print(f"Tasks with ONLY passes: {sum(1 for t, s in task_stats.items() if s['passed'] > 0 and s['failed'] == 0)}")
print(f"Tasks with ONLY fails: {sum(1 for t, s in task_stats.items() if s['passed'] == 0 and s['failed'] > 0)}")
